title: Relate division to multiplication
description: See the relationship between multiplication and division problems.
topic_path: Module 1: Properties of multiplication and division and solving problems with units of 2–5 and 10
Topic E: Multiplication and division using units of 4

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can help us find the unknown number in  $27 \div 3 = \blue\square~?$**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To help us solve a division problem, we can rewrite it as a multiplication problem:

$27\div3 = \blue{\square}$ $~~$ because  $~~$ $\blue\square\times3=27$.
$27\div3 = ~\blue{9}$ $~~$ because  $~~$ $\blue9\times3=27$.
$\blue\square \times 3 = 27$ can help us find the unknown number in  $27 \div 3 = \blue\square$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can help us find the unknown number in $81 \div 9 = \blue\blacklozenge~?$**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To help us solve a division problem, we can rewrite it as a multiplication problem:


$81\div9 = \blue{\blacklozenge}$ $~~$ because  $~~$ $\blue\blacklozenge\times9=81$.
$81\div9 = ~\blue{9}$ $~~$ because  $~~$ $\blue{9}\times9=81$.
$\blue\blacklozenge \times 9 = 81$ can help us find the unknown number in $81 \div 9 = \blue\blacklozenge$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can help us find the unknown number in $63 \div \blue\square= 7~?$**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To help us solve a division problem, we can rewrite it as a multiplication problem:

$63\div\blue\square = 7$ $~~$ because  $~~$ $\blue\square\times7=63$.
$63\div\blue9 = 7$ $~~$ because  $~~$ $\blue9\times7=63$.
$\blue\square \times 7 = 63$ can help us find the unknown number  in $63 \div \blue\square= 7$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can help us find the unknown number in $21 \div \blue\blacklozenge= 7~?$**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To help us solve a division problem, we can rewrite it as a multiplication problem:


$21\div\blue\blacklozenge = 7$ $~~$ because  $~~$ $\blue\blacklozenge\times7=21$.
$21\div\blue3 = 7$ $~~$ because  $~~$ $\blue3\times7=21$.
$\blue\blacklozenge \times 7 = 21$ can help us find the unknown number in $21 \div \blue\blacklozenge= 7.$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can help us find the unknown number in $\blue\square \div 7= 7~?$**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To help us solve a division problem, we can rewrite it as a multiplication problem:


$\blue\square \div 7= 7$ $~~$ because  $~~$ $7\times7=\blue\square$.
$\blue{49} \div 7= 7$ $~~$ because  $~~$ $7\times7=\blue{49}$.
$7\times7=\blue\square$ can help us find the unknown number  in $\blue\square \div 7= 7$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can help us find the unknown number in $\blue\blacklozenge \div 7= 5~?$**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To help us solve a division problem, we can rewrite it as a multiplication problem:


$\blue\blacklozenge \div 7= 5$ $~~$ because  $~~$ $5\times7=\blue\blacklozenge$.
$\blue{35} \div 7= 5$ $~~$ because  $~~$ $5\times7=\blue{35}$.
$5\times7=\blue\blacklozenge$ can help us find the unknown number  in $\blue\blacklozenge \div 7= 5$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equation can help us find the unknown number in $36 \div 6= \blueD{\heartsuit}$ ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To help us solve a division problem, we can rewrite it as a multiplication problem:


$36 \div 6= \blueD{\heartsuit}$ because $6 \times \blueD{\heartsuit} = 36$.
$36 \div 6= \blueD{6}$ because $6 \times \blueD{6} = 36$.
$6 \times \blueD{\heartsuit} = 36$ can help us find the unknown number  in $36 \div 6= \blueD{\heartsuit}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Create an equation to help us find the unknown number in $63 \div 7 = {?}$ .**

$7\times{}$ [[☃ dropdown 4]] $=$ [[☃ dropdown 5]]
hints:
Multiplication and division are related:

$\begin{align}
\greenD{\text{number of groups}} \times \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \times \greenD{\text{number of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \greenD{\text{number of groups}} =\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}
\end{align}$

In this fact family, the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$ is $\purpleC{63}$.

The $\greenD{\text{number of the groups}}$ is $\greenD{7}$.

![Tape diagram showing 63 is equal to 7 equal groups of question mark](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a28441501a8d5d89969258a6c2c7b47ff353fc39)

The $\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$ is ${\maroonD{?}}$.
The equation $\greenD{7} \times {\maroonD{?}} = \purpleC{63}$ can help us find the unknown number.

[[☃ dropdown 6]] $=$ [[☃ dropdown 4]] ${} \times 5$
hints:

$\begin{align}
\greenD{\text{number of groups}} \times \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\

\\\\

\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}
\end{align}$

In this fact family, the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$ is $\purpleC{35}$.

The $\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$ is ${\maroonD{5}}$.

![Tape diagram showing 35 is equal to question mark equal groups of 5](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4f96db1ebfcfd312e3c9924bb8efa202936c3633)

The $\greenD{\text{number of the groups}}$ is $\greenD{?}$.
The equation $\purpleC{35} = {\greenD{?}} \times {\maroonD{5}}$ can help us find the unknown number.

assessment_items.question_content:
Nora wants to use multiplication to solve ${?} = 56 \div 8$.

**Which two multiplication equations can Nora use?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Multiplication and division are related:

$\begin{align}
\greenD{\text{number of groups}} \times \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \times \greenD{\text{number of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \greenD{\text{number of groups}} =\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}
\end{align}$

In ${\greenD{?}} = \purpleC{56} \div \maroonD{8}$, the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$ is $\purpleC{56}$.

The $\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$ is ${\maroonD{8}}$.

![Tape diagram showing 56 is equal to question mark equal groups of 8](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/834895f4de4e564c0fabd0892b3f22122bc75142)

The $\greenD{\text{number of the groups}}$ is $\greenD{?}$.

Nora can use these multiplication equations:

- $\purpleC{56} = \greenD{{?}} \times \maroonD{8}$
- $\purpleC{56} = \maroonD{8} \times \greenD{{?}}$
